target.name: Theseus
target.founder: Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, Sacha Lévy

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Theseus is a seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Carl Schoeller, Ian Laffey, and Sacha Lévy. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies spanning seed and Series A, supplemented by a broader universe of two public comparables and eight private peers including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Flock Safety.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach here. The five direct comparables—Scale AI ($29.0B Series A), Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—yield a median valuation of $300M. This peer set exhibits wide dispersion, ranging from $60M to $29.0B, reflecting variance in stage, product maturity, and investor composition within the Defense & Security sector.

The analysis supports a triangulated valuation range of $255M to $345M, centered on $300M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the peer median. Key uncertainties include the stage mismatch within the peer set (one seed vs. four Series A companies) and the substantial outlier effect of Scale AI's $29B valuation, which suggests either category divergence or market-specific momentum in the sector. Traction metrics, capital efficiency, and customer concentration would be essential to refine this estimate toward the range endpoints.
